votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the logic of summing odd elements at even positions using an iterative approach that matches the recursive Coq definition.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq logic, using an iterative approach to sum odd elements at even indices, and accurately translates the precondition and specification.
The Python code correctly implements the logic of summing odd elements at even positions using an iterative approach that matches the recursive Coq definition.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. sum_odd_in_even_pos_aux iterates through the list, adding elements that are odd (h % 2 != 0) at even indices (current_idx % 2 == 0), matching the Coq logic of (Nat.even idx) && negb (Nat.even h). The precondition and spec functions also match their Coq counterparts for concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. The sum_odd_in_even_pos_aux function iterates through the list, adding elements that are at even indices (idx % 2 == 0) and are odd (h % 2 != 0), which matches the Coq logic of (Nat.even idx) && negb (Nat.even h). The precondition checks for non-empty list, and the spec checks if output equals the computed sum. All concrete behaviors match.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. The sum_odd_in_even_pos_aux function iterates through the list, checking if the index is even (idx % 2 == 0) and the element is odd (h % 2 != 0), which matches Coq's (Nat.even idx) && negb (Nat.even h). The precondition checks for non-empty list, and the spec checks if output equals the computed sum. All concrete behaviors match.
Python sums odd elements at even (0-based) indices with the same logic and starting index as Coq; precondition and spec are mirrored, so results match on all concrete inputs.

Python iterates from index 0, adds elements h when index is even and h is odd, matching Coq’s Nat.even/oddb logic and recursion. Precondition (non-empty list) and spec (output equals implementation) are implemented identically.
Both sum odd elements at 0-based even indices and start indexing at 0; precondition and spec mirror the Coq definitions.
